react-native.title: JavaScript & React Prerequisites
react-native.description: React Native is React for mobile. Before touching the mobile layer, you need solid JavaScript and React fundamentals — components, hooks, and state — everything carries over.
topics: ES6+ — destructuring, spread, arrow functions, modules
react-native.challenge: Build a small React web app (notes app) to solidify your hooks and state management knowledge before moving to mobile.

react-native.title: React Native Core
react-native.description: Set up your dev environment, understand how React Native bridges JavaScript to native code, and master the core components and styling system.
topics: Expo vs bare React Native — when to use each
Core components — View, Text, Image, TextInput, ScrollView, FlatList
StyleSheet API — style objects, platform-specific styles
Flexbox in React Native — differences from web
Metro bundler — fast refresh, module resolution
Debugging — Expo DevTools, React DevTools, Flipper
react-native.challenge: Build a cross-platform mobile UI for your notes app — recreate the layout from scratch using only RN core components and StyleSheet.

react-native.title: Navigation
react-native.description: Mobile apps are multi-screen experiences. React Navigation is the standard — learn stack, tab, and drawer navigators, pass data between screens, and handle deep linking.
topics: React Navigation setup — native stack, dependencies
Stack Navigator — push, pop, goBack, params
Drawer Navigator — gestures, custom drawer content
react-native.challenge: Extend your notes app with full navigation — a tab bar (Notes, Settings), a stack navigator inside Notes for list → detail, and params passed between screens.

react-native.title: State Management
react-native.description: Manage app-wide state cleanly. Choose the right state solution — Zustand for simplicity, Redux Toolkit for complex flows — and persist state between app launches.
topics: Redux Toolkit — createSlice, createAsyncThunk, RTK Query
react-native.challenge: Add Zustand to your notes app with MMKV persistence — notes survive app kills, and a global theme toggle (light/dark) persists across sessions.

react-native.title: Networking & APIs
react-native.description: Connect your mobile app to a backend. Fetch data efficiently, handle caching and background refresh, manage auth tokens, and build real-time features with WebSockets.
topics: Fetch API and Axios in React Native
Auth token storage — SecureStore (Expo) or Keychain
react-native.challenge: Connect your notes app to a REST backend — fetch notes with TanStack Query, store JWT in SecureStore, and add optimistic mutations for create/delete.

react-native.title: Native Device Features
react-native.description: Leverage the hardware. Use Expo SDK and community libraries to access the camera, push notifications, location services, and other native device capabilities.
topics: Linking — opening URLs, phone numbers, maps
react-native.challenge: Add camera capture to your notes app — users can attach a photo to a note using the camera or gallery, with proper permissions handling on both iOS and Android.

react-native.title: Animations & Performance
react-native.description: Mobile users expect buttery-smooth 60fps animations. Use React Native Reanimated for worklet-based animations that run on the UI thread, and optimize lists with FlashList.
topics: Gesture Handler — pan, pinch, tap gestures
React Native Profiler — JS thread vs UI thread
react-native.challenge: Add swipe-to-delete with a smooth Reanimated gesture to your notes list, with a spring animation on the note cards when they enter the list.

react-native.title: Testing & Publishing
react-native.description: Ship your app confidently. Test components and flows with React Native Testing Library, use EAS Build for cloud builds, and publish to both the App Store and Google Play.
topics: React Native Testing Library — render, fireEvent, waitFor
E2E testing with Detox or Maestro
EAS Build — managed vs bare workflow, build profiles
App signing — iOS provisioning profiles, Android keystore
App Store Connect & Google Play Console submission
react-native.challenge: Write component tests for your notes app with RNTL, set up EAS Build, create a production build, and follow the App Store/Play Store submission checklist.

react-native.title: Advanced Native Integration and New Architecture
react-native.description: Write custom native modules and turbo modules using the JSI layer to eliminate the bridge bottleneck and unlock synchronous JavaScript-to-native calls. Understand the Fabric renderer and how the new React Native architecture changes component and layout pipelines.
topics: JSI fundamentals and synchronous native calls
Turbo Modules authoring for iOS and Android
Fabric renderer and concurrent rendering on mobile
Bridging third-party native SDKs into RN
Hermes engine internals and bytecode optimization
Debugging native code with Xcode and Android Studio

resources.label: Advanced React Native by William Candillon

react-native.challenge: Create a Turbo Module that exposes a platform-specific biometric authentication API to JavaScript, wire it through Codegen, and benchmark call latency compared to the legacy bridge module.

react-native.title: CI/CD, OTA Updates and Production Release Management
react-native.description: Establish a full release pipeline for React Native apps covering automated builds, over-the-air update channels, crash monitoring, and staged rollouts. Learn how to manage multiple environments, signing configurations, and app store submissions without manual steps.
topics: EAS Build and EAS Submit for automated store uploads
OTA update channels and rollback strategies with EAS Update
Fastlane lanes for certificate and profile management
Crash reporting and source-map upload with Sentry
GitHub Actions workflows for RN mono-repo builds
Feature flag rollout and A/B testing on mobile
App store review guidelines and rejection remediation

resources.label: React Native CI/CD with GitHub Actions

react-native.challenge: Configure an EAS project with three update channels (development, staging, production), automate TestFlight and Play Internal Track uploads via GitHub Actions, and verify Sentry receives symbolicated crash reports.

flutter.title: Dart Fundamentals
flutter.description: Flutter apps are written in Dart. Learn Dart's syntax, type system with null safety, and async programming before building your first widget.
topics: Functions — named params, arrow functions, closures
Collections — List, Map, Set with generics
flutter.challenge: Build a Dart CLI app that models a simple bank account — deposit, withdraw, balance history — demonstrating OOP, null safety, and async operations.

flutter.title: Flutter UI Fundamentals
flutter.description: In Flutter, everything is a widget. Learn the widget tree, layout system, and Material Design 3 to build beautiful, responsive UIs from scratch.
topics: Layout — Row, Column, Stack, Flex, Expanded, Padding
Common widgets — Text, Container, Image, Icon, AppBar
flutter.challenge: Build a shopping app home screen in Flutter — responsive grid of product cards, a search bar AppBar, bottom navigation, and a custom color theme.

flutter.title: State Management
flutter.description: Flutter's reactive UI is powered by state. Learn when to use setState, Provider, and Riverpod 2.0 — the modern solution for scalable Flutter state management.
topics: setState — when it's enough and when it's not
Shared state patterns — dependency injection with Riverpod
flutter.challenge: Rewrite your shopping app with Riverpod — a cart provider, a product list with async fetch state (loading/error/data), and a global theme toggle.

flutter.title: Navigation & Routing
flutter.description: Learn Flutter's navigation system from the imperative Navigator API to the modern declarative GoRouter with deep linking support.
topics: GoRouter — route definitions, path params, redirect guards
Deep linking — Android intent filters, iOS URL schemes
Auth guards — redirect to login if not authenticated
flutter.challenge: Add GoRouter to your shopping app — product list → product detail route with path params, a protected checkout route, and a bottom tab shell.

flutter.title: Networking & Firebase
flutter.description: Connect your Flutter app to backend services. Use Dio for typed HTTP requests, Firebase for real-time data and auth, and Firestore for a serverless database.
topics: Firebase setup — FlutterFire CLI, platform config
Cloud Firestore — collections, queries, real-time listeners
flutter.challenge: Connect your shopping app to Firestore — real products from a Firestore collection, Firebase Auth for login, and a real-time cart shared across devices.

flutter.title: Local Storage & Native Features
flutter.description: Access device capabilities — store data locally, use the camera, send push notifications, and leverage platform-specific native APIs.
topics: Hive or Isar for local structured data
flutter.challenge: Add offline support to your shopping app — cache the product list in Hive, add a profile photo using image_picker, and send a local notification when an order is placed.

flutter.title: Testing & Performance
flutter.description: Test your widgets and user flows, then profile and optimize rendering performance using Flutter DevTools.
topics: Unit tests — testing Riverpod providers, pure logic
Widget tests — pumpWidget, finder, tap, enter text
Flutter DevTools — widget inspector, performance overlay

resources.label: Very Good Ventures — Flutter Testing Guide

flutter.challenge: Write widget tests for your shopping app's cart — add to cart, remove, and total price. Run the Flutter performance overlay and eliminate any jank on the product list scroll.

flutter.title: Publishing & CI/CD
flutter.description: Ship your Flutter app to both stores. Automate builds and releases with Fastlane and GitHub Actions, and navigate the App Store and Play Store submission process.
topics: iOS — provisioning profiles, certificates, App Store Connect
Android — keystore, Play Console, aab build
GitHub Actions for Flutter — build, test, deploy
flutter.challenge: Set up a GitHub Actions pipeline that runs tests, builds release APK and IPA, and uses Fastlane to publish to TestFlight and the Play Store internal track.

flutter.title: Advanced Flutter - Custom Rendering and Multi-Platform Targets
flutter.description: Go beyond standard widgets by implementing custom render objects and painters for pixel-perfect UI needs that the widget layer cannot express. Extend Flutter to desktop and web targets while bridging platform-specific native SDKs through platform channels.
topics: Custom RenderObject and RenderBox from scratch
CustomPainter and Canvas API for complex graphics
Platform channels and MethodChannel for native SDK bridging
Flutter Web with CanvasKit vs HTML renderer trade-offs
Desktop window management and keyboard/mouse input handling
FFI with dart:ffi for calling native C libraries
Isolate communication for CPU-intensive render work

resources.label: Advanced Flutter - Custom Widgets and Rendering

flutter.challenge: Build a chart widget using a fully custom RenderObject with hit-testing, animate it with an AnimationController, expose it as a platform channel to swap the data source from a native SQL query on iOS and Android.

flutter.title: Production Flutter Apps - Analytics, A/B Testing and Store Optimization
flutter.description: Operate Flutter apps in production by integrating crash reporting, event analytics, and controlled feature rollouts that let you validate changes without full store releases. Learn how to manage code-push updates, optimize store listings, and use data to drive product decisions.
topics: Crash reporting and stack trace symbolication with Firebase Crashlytics
Event analytics schema design and funnel tracking
A/B testing experiments with Firebase Remote Config
Shorebird code-push for Dart-layer OTA updates
App Store and Play Store listing optimization (ASO)
Staged rollouts and phased releases on both stores
Compliance with store policies for data privacy and permissions
flutter.challenge: Integrate Crashlytics and Firebase Analytics into an existing Flutter app, set up two Remote Config A/B test variants for an onboarding screen, deploy a Shorebird patch to fix a UI bug, and verify the fix via the Shorebird dashboard without a store update.

ios.title: Swift Fundamentals
ios.description: Swift is Apple's powerful, type-safe language for iOS, macOS, and beyond. Learn its syntax, type system, protocol-oriented programming, and Swift concurrency.
topics: Variables — let/var, type inference, optionals ?, !, guard let
Functions — default params, closures, trailing closure syntax
OOP — classes, structs, enums with associated values
Error handling — throws, try/catch, Result type

resources.label: Hacking with Swift — 100 Days of Swift

ios.challenge: Build a Swift CLI app that models a social media timeline — structs for Post/User, a feed algorithm (sorted by engagement), and async fetching from a mock API.

ios.title: SwiftUI Fundamentals
ios.description: SwiftUI is Apple's declarative UI framework. Learn to think in Views, understand the state-driven rendering model, and compose interfaces with modifiers.
topics: Layout — HStack, VStack, ZStack, LazyVStack, Grid
Common views — Text, Image, Button, TextField, List, Form
View modifiers — padding, background, cornerRadius, overlay

resources.label: Hacking with Swift — 100 Days of SwiftUI

ios.challenge: Build a SwiftUI contacts app — List of contacts, search bar, add contact sheet, detail view, and a custom color theme.

ios.title: Architecture — MVVM & Combine
ios.description: Structure your SwiftUI apps for maintainability with MVVM, reactive data flow using Combine (or the new @Observable macro in iOS 17+), and clean separation of concerns.
topics: Swift Concurrency in ViewModels — Task, MainActor

resources.label: Apple Developer — Data Flow in SwiftUI

ios.challenge: Refactor your contacts app with a ViewModel using @Observable, a ContactsRepository protocol, and dependency-injected mock for previews.

ios.title: Data Persistence — SwiftData & Core Data
ios.description: Persist data locally on iOS with SwiftData (iOS 17+ modern ORM), Core Data for older targets, and UserDefaults/Keychain for lightweight storage.
topics: CloudKit + Core Data for iCloud sync
ios.challenge: Add SwiftData persistence to your contacts app — contacts survive app restarts, enable iCloud sync with CloudKit, and store sensitive notes in the Keychain.

ios.title: Networking & Async
ios.description: Fetch data from REST APIs using URLSession with Swift Concurrency. Decode with Codable, handle errors gracefully, and cache responses efficiently.

resources.label: Donny Wals — Modern Networking in Swift

ios.challenge: Add a REST API layer to your contacts app — fetch contacts from a mock server, decode with Codable, cache with SwiftData, and show loading/error states.

ios.title: Navigation & Advanced SwiftUI
ios.description: Navigate between views using the modern NavigationStack API, build iPad-ready split views, and add fluid animations with SwiftUI's animation system.
ios.challenge: Add NavigationStack with a contacts list → detail → edit flow, animate the card entrance with matchedGeometryEffect, and add a sidebar layout for iPad.

ios.title: Testing iOS Apps
ios.description: Write unit, integration, and UI tests for your iOS app using XCTest and the new Swift Testing framework introduced in Xcode 16.
topics: Code coverage in Xcode — measuring and improving
ios.challenge: Write unit tests for your ViewModel, mock the ContactsRepository, test a user flow end-to-end with XCUITest, and achieve >80% code coverage.

ios.title: App Store Publishing
ios.description: Ship your iOS app to the App Store. Configure code signing, test with TestFlight, automate with Xcode Cloud or Fastlane, and navigate the App Review process.
topics: Code signing — certificates, provisioning profiles, Apple Developer
App capabilities — Push Notifications, iCloud, In-App Purchases
TestFlight — internal and external beta testing
Xcode Cloud — CI/CD built into Xcode
App Store metadata — screenshots, ASO, privacy labels
App Review guidelines — common rejection reasons
ios.challenge: Set up Xcode Cloud to run your test suite on every push, build a TestFlight build on merge to main, and complete the App Store Connect submission form with screenshots.

ios.title: Advanced iOS - Extensions, Widgets and Background Tasks
ios.description: Extend your iOS apps beyond the main process using WidgetKit for home and lock-screen surfaces, App Clips for instant experiences, and BGTaskScheduler for deferred background work. Implement push notifications with rich media payloads and notification service extensions.
topics: WidgetKit timeline providers and widget families
Lock Screen widgets and StandBy mode complications
App Clips entitlements, size limits, and invocation URLs
Push notification service extensions for media attachments
Share and action extensions with App Groups data sharing

resources.label: WWDC - Complications and Widgets Reuse with SwiftUI
iOS 17 Programming for Beginners - Advanced Chapters

ios.challenge: Build a WidgetKit widget with three sizes that reads data from an App Group shared by the main app, schedule a BGProcessingTask to refresh that data nightly, and send a push notification with a thumbnail image using a notification service extension.

ios.title: Production iOS - StoreKit 2, Analytics and App Store Optimization
ios.description: Ship and monetize iOS apps professionally by implementing StoreKit 2 for modern in-app purchases and subscriptions, integrating crash reporting with symbolicated stack traces, and running hypothesis-driven A/B tests through Firebase. Master App Store Connect metrics and listing optimization to improve conversion and discoverability.
topics: StoreKit 2 Transaction API and subscription lifecycle management
Server-side receipt verification with App Store Server API
Firebase Crashlytics dSYM upload and crash-free rate targets
Firebase A/B Testing with Remote Config parameters
App Store Connect analytics - impressions, conversion, retention
App Store listing optimization - keywords, screenshots, preview videos
Xcode Cloud workflows for TestFlight and phased App Store releases
ios.challenge: Implement a StoreKit 2 annual subscription with a promotional offer, verify transactions server-side using the App Store Server API, upload dSYMs to Crashlytics via an Xcode Cloud post-action script, and set up a Firebase A/B test comparing two paywall layouts tracking a purchase conversion event.

android.title: Kotlin Fundamentals
android.description: Kotlin is the official language for Android development. It's concise, null-safe, and has excellent coroutine support. Master it before writing your first Activity.
topics: Variables — val/var, type inference, null safety ?, !!
Functions — default params, extension functions, lambdas
OOP — data classes, sealed classes, objects, companion
Collections — List, Map, Set, higher-order functions
Scope functions — let, run, apply, also, with
android.challenge: Build a Kotlin CLI app that parses a CSV file and generates a statistical summary — mean, median, and mode — using coroutines for file I/O.

android.title: Jetpack Compose UI
android.description: Jetpack Compose is Android's modern declarative UI toolkit. Learn composable functions, the state-driven UI model, and Material Design 3 components.
topics: Material 3 components — Scaffold, TopAppBar, Card, Button
android.challenge: Build a Compose UI for a to-do app — LazyColumn list, add/delete/complete tasks, a TopAppBar with a filter menu, and a FAB.

android.title: Architecture — MVVM & Clean
android.description: Android apps need a clear architecture to stay maintainable. Learn MVVM with ViewModels, StateFlow, and dependency injection with Hilt.
topics: Clean Architecture layers — data, domain, presentation
android.challenge: Restructure your to-do app with MVVM + Hilt — a ViewModel with StateFlow, a repository interface, use cases for add/delete/filter, and proper DI.

android.title: Local Storage — Room & DataStore
android.description: Persist data on the device with Room (SQLite ORM) for structured data and DataStore for preferences — both with full coroutine and Flow support.
android.challenge: Migrate your to-do app to Room — DAO with insert/update/delete/query, reactive Flow from queries, a DataStore preference for the selected theme.

android.title: Networking with Retrofit & Coroutines
android.description: Fetch data from the internet with Retrofit, parse JSON with Kotlin Serialization, cache images with Coil, and handle errors and loading states cleanly.
topics: Sealed class Result<T> for API responses
Coil for image loading in Compose
android.challenge: Add a REST API layer to your to-do app — sync tasks with a backend, load user avatars with Coil, and implement offline caching with Room as the single source of truth.

android.title: Navigation & Deep Links
android.description: Navigate between screens, pass arguments, and handle deep links with Jetpack Navigation for Compose.
topics: Bottom navigation bar with Navigation Compose
Deep links — intent filters, navigation links
android.challenge: Add bottom navigation to your to-do app (Tasks, Stats, Settings tabs), deep link to a specific task from a notification, and animate between screens.

android.title: Testing Android Apps
android.description: Test your ViewModel logic with JUnit, your Compose UI with the Compose testing APIs, and your coroutine flows with Turbine.
topics: JUnit 4/5 — unit testing ViewModels, use cases
Turbine — testing StateFlow and Flow emissions
Compose UI testing — composeTestRule, semantics, actions
android.challenge: Write tests covering your ViewModel state transitions, DAO queries with in-memory Room, and two Compose UI tests for adding and deleting a task.

android.title: Publishing to Google Play
android.description: Ship your Android app to the world. Configure Gradle for release builds, shrink code with R8/ProGuard, and automate deployment with GitHub Actions and Fastlane.
topics: Gradle — build variants, product flavors, signing config
Google Play Console — internal, alpha, beta, production tracks
Fastlane supply — automated Play Store deployment
GitHub Actions — build signed AAB, upload to Play
android.challenge: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that builds a signed release AAB, runs your test suite, and automatically uploads to the Play Store internal track on main branch merge.

android.title: Advanced Android - Background Processing and System Integration
android.description: Build rich system integrations using WorkManager for guaranteed background tasks, advanced notification channels, and app widgets powered by the Glance API. Explore Wear OS fundamentals and understand how Android manages process priority and battery restrictions.
topics: WorkManager chains, constraints, and expedited tasks
Notification channels, rich notifications, and bubbles
Foreground services and battery optimization compliance
App widgets with Glance and RemoteViews
Wear OS tile and complication development basics
Doze mode, App Standby buckets, and JobScheduler interaction
android.challenge: Implement a WorkManager periodic task that syncs data in the background respecting network and charging constraints, surfaces progress via a foreground service notification, and updates a Glance widget with the last sync timestamp.

android.title: Production Android - Store Optimization, Billing and Feature Flags
android.description: Maximize app success on the Play Store by integrating Play Billing Library for subscriptions, using Firebase to run controlled experiments, and applying crash analytics to triage regressions before they reach a wide audience. Learn listing optimization tactics and phased rollout strategies.
topics: Play Billing Library 6 - subscriptions and one-time products
Purchase verification and server-side receipt validation
Firebase Crashlytics ANR and crash triage workflow
Remote Config feature flags and parameter hygiene
A/B testing with Firebase A/B Testing and Grow experiments
Play Store listing optimization - screenshots, short description, keywords
Phased rollouts and halt-and-resume release management
android.challenge: Add a monthly subscription product using Play Billing Library with server-side purchase token verification, gate the premium feature behind a Firebase Remote Config flag, run a 50/50 A/B test on the paywall copy, and monitor the conversion funnel in Firebase Analytics.

kotlin-dev.title: Kotlin Fundamentals
kotlin-dev.description: Start with Kotlin's expressive, concise syntax on the JVM. Learn the basics that make Kotlin a joy to write.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Build a CLI expense tracker that reads input and prints categorized totals.

kotlin-dev.title: OOP & Null Safety
kotlin-dev.description: Model your domain with Kotlin's classes and embrace null safety — the feature that kills NullPointerExceptions.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Model a domain (orders, items, customers) with data and sealed classes and zero nullable misuse.

kotlin-dev.title: Functional Kotlin & Collections
kotlin-dev.description: Write idiomatic, functional Kotlin. Master higher-order functions and the rich collections API.
topics: Scope functions — let, run, apply, also, with
kotlin-dev.challenge: Process a dataset with pure collection pipelines and write three useful extension functions.

kotlin-dev.title: Coroutines & Concurrency
kotlin-dev.description: Concurrency the Kotlin way. Write asynchronous, non-blocking code with coroutines and structured concurrency.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Build a concurrent feed aggregator that fetches multiple sources in parallel with structured concurrency.

kotlin-dev.title: Flow & Async Streams
kotlin-dev.description: Handle streams of values over time with Kotlin Flow — the reactive backbone of modern Kotlin apps.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Model a live ticker as a Flow with operators and expose current state through a StateFlow.

kotlin-dev.title: Build Tooling with Gradle
kotlin-dev.description: Every real Kotlin project is built with Gradle. Learn the build lifecycle, the Kotlin DSL and dependency management.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Convert your project to a multi-module Gradle build using the Kotlin DSL and a version catalog.

kotlin-dev.title: Backend with Ktor
kotlin-dev.description: Build asynchronous backend services with Ktor, JetBrains' lightweight Kotlin-first web framework.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Build a JSON REST API with Ktor for a tasks service, with routing and serialization.

kotlin-dev.title: Data & Persistence
kotlin-dev.description: Persist data from your Ktor backend using Exposed, JetBrains' Kotlin SQL framework, against PostgreSQL.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Back your tasks API with PostgreSQL via Exposed, with a connection pool and transactional writes.

kotlin-dev.title: Testing in Kotlin
kotlin-dev.description: Test Kotlin code idiomatically. Use JUnit5, MockK and Kotest to cover units, coroutines and endpoints.
kotlin-dev.challenge: Write unit tests with MockK and Kotest plus Ktor endpoint tests for your tasks API.

kotlin-dev.title: Multiplatform & Deployment
kotlin-dev.description: Share Kotlin across targets and ship it. Explore Kotlin Multiplatform and deploy your Ktor service.
topics: Building a fat jar / native image
kotlin-dev.challenge: Containerize your Ktor service, add a CI pipeline, and extract shared logic into a multiplatform module.

swift-dev.title: Swift Fundamentals
swift-dev.description: Learn Swift, Apple's modern, safe and expressive language. Start with syntax and core types in Xcode Playgrounds.
swift-dev.challenge: Build a Playground tip calculator that handles different bill amounts and split sizes.

swift-dev.title: Types, Optionals & Error Handling
swift-dev.description: Master Swift's safety features. Use optionals to model absence and handle failures with typed errors.
swift-dev.challenge: Build a parser that decodes user input into an enum model and reports failures with typed errors.

swift-dev.title: Protocols, Generics & Value Semantics
swift-dev.description: Write flexible, reusable Swift with protocol-oriented programming, generics and an understanding of value semantics.
swift-dev.challenge: Design a generic caching protocol with two conforming implementations and value-type models.

swift-dev.title: Memory & Modern Concurrency
swift-dev.description: Understand how Swift manages memory and write safe concurrent code with async/await and actors.
topics: Retain cycles & how to break them
swift-dev.challenge: Refactor a callback-based downloader to async/await and isolate shared state in an actor.

swift-dev.title: SwiftUI Fundamentals
swift-dev.description: Build declarative UIs with SwiftUI. Compose views, lay them out, and react to data automatically.
swift-dev.challenge: Build a multi-screen recipe browser UI in SwiftUI with navigation and a detail view.

swift-dev.title: SwiftUI State & Data Flow
swift-dev.description: Master how data drives SwiftUI. Use the state property wrappers and the observation system to build dynamic apps.
swift-dev.challenge: Add state, forms and animated transitions so users can create and edit recipes in your app.

swift-dev.title: Combine & Reactive Programming
swift-dev.description: Handle asynchronous events the reactive way with Combine — Apple's framework for processing values over time.
topics: Combine vs async/await — when to use which
swift-dev.challenge: Build a live search-as-you-type feature using Combine with debouncing and cancellation.

swift-dev.title: Networking & Persistence
swift-dev.description: Connect to APIs and store data locally. Use URLSession with Codable and persist with SwiftData.
swift-dev.challenge: Fetch recipes from a REST API with Codable and persist favorites locally with SwiftData.

swift-dev.title: Testing & Debugging
swift-dev.description: Ship reliable Swift. Write unit and UI tests, and profile your app with Instruments.
swift-dev.challenge: Add unit tests for your networking and persistence layers plus one UI test for the create flow.

swift-dev.title: Swift Packages & Deployment
swift-dev.description: Package and ship Swift beyond a single app. Build reusable packages, try server-side Swift, and distribute.
swift-dev.challenge: Extract reusable logic into a Swift package and stand up a small Vapor API it can share.

maui.title: C# Fundamentals
maui.description: MAUI is built on C# and .NET. Start with the language fundamentals and the .NET runtime.
maui.challenge: Build a CLI unit converter in C# that validates input and handles errors.

maui.title: Object-Oriented C# & .NET
maui.description: Model your app with object-oriented C#. Learn classes, interfaces and how to use the .NET ecosystem.
maui.challenge: Design a small domain model with interfaces and wire it up with .NET dependency injection.

maui.title: Async & LINQ
maui.description: Write responsive, data-driven C#. Master async/await for non-blocking code and LINQ for querying collections.
maui.challenge: Fetch and transform JSON from a public API asynchronously and query the results with LINQ.

maui.title: .NET MAUI Basics
maui.description: Build your first cross-platform app. Learn the MAUI project structure, pages and controls that run on iOS, Android, macOS and Windows.
maui.challenge: Build a multi-page MAUI app shell that runs on Android and Windows.

maui.title: XAML & UI Layout
maui.description: Design polished UIs declaratively with XAML. Master layouts, styling and reusable controls.
maui.challenge: Build a responsive product detail screen in XAML with a reusable style dictionary.

maui.title: MVVM & Data Binding
maui.description: Structure MAUI apps the right way. Adopt the MVVM pattern with data binding and the MVVM Community Toolkit.
maui.challenge: Refactor your product screen to MVVM with bound view models and commands.

maui.title: Navigation & Platform Features
maui.description: Build full apps with structured navigation and access to native device capabilities.
maui.challenge: Add Shell-based navigation and use the device geolocation API with proper permission handling.

maui.title: Data & Networking
maui.description: Connect your app to the cloud and store data locally. Consume REST APIs and persist with SQLite.
maui.challenge: Add REST sync and local SQLite caching so your app works offline and updates when online.

maui.title: Testing & Debugging
maui.description: Ship reliable MAUI apps. Unit-test your view models and debug across platforms.
maui.challenge: Write xUnit tests for your view models and mock the network layer.

maui.title: Azure, Distribution & CI/CD
maui.description: Take your app to production. Add cloud services, automate builds, and publish to the app stores.
topics: CI/CD with GitHub Actions / Azure DevOps
maui.challenge: Wire an Azure Function backend, set up CI to build signed apps, and publish a test release.

pwa-dev.title: Web Foundations
pwa-dev.description: A PWA is a website first. Solidify the HTML, CSS and HTTP foundations that everything else builds on.
topics: Why HTTPS is required for PWAs
pwa-dev.challenge: Build a fast, accessible, responsive single-page site served over HTTPS.

pwa-dev.title: Modern JavaScript
pwa-dev.description: PWAs are driven by JavaScript. Master modern language features and asynchronous patterns.
pwa-dev.challenge: Build a weather app that fetches live data and handles loading and error states.

pwa-dev.title: Responsive & App-Shell UI
pwa-dev.description: Make your web app feel native. Build a responsive app shell that loads instantly and adapts to any device.
pwa-dev.challenge: Build an app-shell layout with instant navigation and skeleton loading states.

pwa-dev.title: The Web App Manifest
pwa-dev.description: Make your app installable. Configure the web app manifest so users can add it to their home screen.
pwa-dev.challenge: Add a complete manifest with maskable icons and a custom install prompt to your app.

pwa-dev.title: Service Workers
pwa-dev.description: The heart of every PWA. Master the service worker lifecycle and intercept network requests.
pwa-dev.challenge: Register a service worker that intercepts requests and serves a custom offline page.

pwa-dev.title: Caching Strategies & Offline
pwa-dev.description: Make your app work offline. Apply caching strategies and store data locally for a resilient experience.
pwa-dev.challenge: Make your weather app fully usable offline with Workbox caching and IndexedDB storage.

pwa-dev.title: Push Notifications & Background Sync
pwa-dev.description: Re-engage users like a native app. Add push notifications and sync data in the background.
pwa-dev.challenge: Add push notifications with a VAPID-secured backend and queue failed requests with Background Sync.

pwa-dev.title: Device & Web APIs
pwa-dev.description: Tap into device capabilities from the browser. Use the modern web platform APIs that close the gap with native.
pwa-dev.challenge: Add camera capture and Web Share to your app, with graceful fallbacks where unsupported.

pwa-dev.title: Performance & Lighthouse
pwa-dev.description: Fast is a feature. Measure and optimize your PWA against Core Web Vitals using Lighthouse.
topics: Core Web Vitals — LCP, INP, CLS
pwa-dev.challenge: Get your PWA to a 90+ Lighthouse score across performance, PWA and best-practices audits.

pwa-dev.title: Packaging & Deployment
pwa-dev.description: Ship your PWA everywhere. Deploy it, and optionally package it for the app stores.
topics: Deploying to a CDN / static host
Packaging for stores with PWABuilder / TWA
pwa-dev.challenge: Deploy your PWA to a CDN and package it for the Play Store using a Trusted Web Activity.

ionic.title: Web & TypeScript Foundations
ionic.description: Ionic builds on web tech. Get comfortable with HTML, CSS and TypeScript before adding the framework.
ionic.challenge: Build a small typed to-do UI in plain TypeScript and the DOM.

ionic.title: Angular Fundamentals
ionic.description: Ionic's most common companion is Angular. Learn components, services and reactive data with RxJS.
ionic.challenge: Build an Angular app with routing, a service, and a reactive form.

ionic.title: Ionic Framework & Components
ionic.description: Build native-looking mobile UIs with Ionic's component library that adapts to iOS and Android.
ionic.challenge: Build a tabbed mobile UI using Ionic components with custom theming.

ionic.title: Navigation & Routing
ionic.description: Structure your app's flows. Master Ionic navigation, tabs, modals and route parameters.
ionic.challenge: Add tabbed navigation, a detail route with params, and a modal create flow.

ionic.title: State Management
ionic.description: Manage app state cleanly. Use services, RxJS and signals to keep your UI in sync with data.
ionic.challenge: Build a shared state service with RxJS that keeps two screens in sync.

ionic.title: Native with Capacitor
ionic.description: Turn your web app into a real native app. Use Capacitor to build for iOS and Android.
ionic.challenge: Wrap your Ionic app with Capacitor and run it on an Android emulator and an iOS simulator.

ionic.title: Device APIs & Plugins
ionic.description: Access native device features. Use Capacitor plugins for camera, geolocation, storage and more.
ionic.challenge: Add photo capture and geolocation to your app using Capacitor plugins.

ionic.title: Data, HTTP & Storage
ionic.description: Connect your app to a backend and persist data. Consume APIs, store data locally, and handle auth.
topics: Local storage with Ionic Storage / SQLite
ionic.challenge: Add token-based auth and local caching so the app loads instantly and survives offline.

ionic.title: Testing & Debugging
ionic.description: Keep your app reliable. Test Angular/Ionic code and debug on real devices.
ionic.challenge: Write unit tests for your services and components and debug a layout issue on a device.

ionic.title: Building & Publishing
ionic.description: Release your Ionic app to the world. Build signed binaries and publish to the app stores.
ionic.challenge: Produce signed iOS and Android builds and complete a test submission to both stores.
